title: Question about manga
wikitext:
[]: Question about manga             Quote Post by zlink64 » Tue Nov 18, 2008 12:56 pm How close is the manga to the show? is it a retelling or a interpation of the story by the artist writing it? And how involed is anno in the mangas story. And how involed is the artist guy in the Story of the show (not the art part)? Just want to know if they're the same thing or two seprate storys using the same characters and themes.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Tue Nov 18, 2008 2:25 pm The story for the manga is completely written by the character designer of the show - Yoshiyuki Sadamoto. Anno is not involved, but gave his full blessin to Sadamoto to give his own interpretation of the story. The basic plot is the same, but there are some very interesting (read 'blasphemous' if you're a purist) changes. Shinji is the same basic character, but is less of a 'wimpy' person, and more angry. There is a lot less focus on Asuka. A hint of romance developes between Shinji and Rei. Kaji has a backstory. Etc. Hope that's a good enough explanation

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 18, 2008 4:29 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: The story for the manga is completely written by the character designer of the show - Yoshiyuki Sadamoto.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Mon Nov 24, 2008 7:41 pm - Rei's telepathic powers are less ambiguously demonstrated - Kaworu is a kitty-killing bastard - fewer Angels/less filler Little things like that

[]:             Quote Post by M2Dane » Wed Dec 17, 2008 2:30 pm They're a lot less angels. Sandolphon Matareal Ireul Leliel Have all been skipped over. I do enjoy reading the manga, though. I still am waiting on my 11th English Volume to arrive.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Thu Dec 18, 2008 4:44 pm It's not a big loss since most of these angels were just useless. Anyway, I like the manga, but I really hate its dialogues.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Thu Dec 18, 2008 7:56 pm It does screw with personalities and backstories quite a bit though. Asuka's now a test-tube baby, and Shinji is rather aggressive. He even tries to punch Gendou. And Kaworu ceases to be a sympathetic character and is instead a kitten-killing outright homosexual. Also: It started slightly before the animu did, and still isn't finished. Sadamoto hasn't put out a new chapter in months.

[]:             Quote Post by Midori » Sat Dec 20, 2008 7:02 pm I read the manga before I saw the show, so my interperatation of the characters mainly comes from the books. Although there are some divergenses in the plot, you pretty much get the same story, it't just a little different how you get there. Example (may cause spoiler to episode 9 and volume 4): In the original ep 9, "Both of You Dance Like You Want to Win", Shinji and Asuka need to live together to "harmonize" in order to beat the Angel in 5 days. In the manga (vol 4) they stay at headquarters, rather than Misato's house like in the show, there are some variations in jokes, and in the manga the characters dont use the effed up DDR thing to train. But, in the end, it's the same basic premise and final battle (in which they are victorius). Also, you seem to see the characters "true colors" a little more in the manga, primarily because they are not only more expressive, but, the very obvious reason is because you can read their thoughts and narrarations. The only time you see this in the show is during an akward voice over. Because of the whole 'reading thoughts" and "more expressive" deal, the manga seems to me to be a little more conrete than the show. Meaning, in the show, almost everything is up for your own interpretation and can sometimes be a little harder to understand, while in the manga, although equally as complicated, offers a more "set-in-stone" view. But in the end, it's the same, amazing, wild story line we all love. (Please note though, the manga is not yet completed. There are allegedly going to be 12 volumes, and vol 11 just came out in the US last month. About three quarters into vol 11, we start with the "end" of the series. But rather than follow the original ending, eps 25 and 26, it starts to more or less follow the movie End of Evangelion. So far, the scanlations tell me it's going down the path of EoE. But since no new chapters have been up for a while, and given EVA's infamous past with it's ending, we really have no idea how the manga will end, if it will be the same or similar to EoE, or maybe revert to the original ending, or maybe we'll get something completly new)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 20, 2008 7:11 pm Midori wrote: Example (may cause spoiler to episode 9 and volume 4):

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Sat Dec 20, 2008 7:29 pm And Anime-Shinji is a boy who feels distinctly worthless and can't do anything for himself. Manga-Shinji is an angsty teen, as per his violent outbursts at Gendou and his constant sarcastic thoughts.

[]:             Quote Post by Midori » Sat Dec 20, 2008 8:00 pm Shiro wrote: And Anime-Shinji is a boy who feels distinctly worthless and can't do anything for himself. Manga-Shinji is an angsty teen, as per his violent outbursts at Gendou and his constant sarcastic thoughts.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 20, 2008 8:35 pm Just fyi, supposedly 13 volumes of the manga total will be released.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Dec 21, 2008 7:21 am Midori wrote: Also, you seem to see the characters "true colors" a little more in the manga, primarily because they are not only more expressive, but, the very obvious reason is because you can read their thoughts and narrarations. The only time you see this in the show is during an akward voice over.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 21, 2008 7:51 am Hunter21 wrote: I feel soiled

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Dec 21, 2008 10:09 am Count A'ight wrote: - Rei's telepathic powers are less ambiguously demonstrated

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 21, 2008 12:34 pm V wrote: specific example?
